RAMP's 72% high-prescience rate vs DCT's 20% reflects methodology design, not analyst accuracy: RAMP interviews force multi-year extrapolation (structurally available for high-prescience grading); DCT weekly tracking forces days-to-weeks tactical calls (structurally unavailable for multi-year grading).

metric_value: Kastner 2002-2003 DCT commentary treated Mira as the first credible kitchen-screen form factor.
Microsoft killed Smart Display in 2003; category did not return until iPad and Windows 8 tablets in 2010-2012.
Both refuted predictions share the failure mode of overestimating how quickly a technically-superior alternative can unseat a good-enough installed base.
notes: Key lesson applicable to AI cycle today.

The mid-line storage white paper's core claim — a new SATA-based tier between high-end FC and DAS would become mainstream enterprise storage capacity — turned out to be the dominant storage story of the decade.
notes: NetApp FAS, EMC CLARiiON CX, Dell/EMC mid-tier confirmed it.

metric_value: Weekly retail tracker captured vendor-by-vendor consumer PC shifts in real time (Gateway weakness, eMachines over Sony entry-level, Dell direct squeezing HP and Compaq) that no interview methodology could have captured at that velocity.
Market-timing prescience (when tech arrives, in what form) vs consumer-utility prescience (whether tech actually matters to users).
RAMP is structurally strong on market-timing prescience for enterprise buyers; the methodology forces the timing question.

DCT is structurally strong on consumer-utility prescience; weekly retail tracking forces the utility question — will this product move at this price this week — which is the same question, compressed in time, that determines whether a category eventually matters.

metric_name: Lesson: DCT should have had 24-month outcome follow-ups
metric_value: Weekly cadence did not naturally produce a retrospective; outcomes sat as [UNVERIFIED] for 23 years until 2026 Phase 3 backfill.

metric_name: Lesson: DCT should have separated tactical notes from structural calls
metric_value: Mixing tactical ('Circuit City slow on Athlon 64') and structural ('UWB is next home-video transport') inside a single weekly commentary flattened the confidence signal; RAMP's interview instrument separated them naturally.
AI incumbents today have installed-base advantages similar to 2003 Wi-Fi and 2003 laptops; technically-superior alternatives are not winning bets by default.
notes: Forward-looking application of the common-failure-mode analysis.

metric_value: Both methodologies worked, both were right about what they were designed to see, and the two refutations shared a single failure mode: overestimating incumbent-displacement speed.
